claim.observation: Volatility3 windows.netscan output shows multiple TCPv4 rows whose local address is 172.16.7.11 connecting outbound to several distinct internal hosts/ports: an ESTABLISHED session 172.16.7.11:56150 -> 172.16.5.21:5985, plus CLOSED rows to 172.16.5.21:5985, 172.16.4.10:8080 (repeated), 172.16.4.4:135, 172.16.5.20:443, and 172.16.5.25:5682. All of these rows show PID and Owner as '-'.
claim.interpretation: The workstation (local IP 172.16.7.11) initiated outbound TCP connections to multiple distinct internal hosts and service ports, including an ESTABLISHED connection to 172.16.5.21 on port 5985 (WS-Management / WinRM, used for remote command execution) and repeated connections to 172.16.4.10 on 8080, plus 172.16.4.4:135 (RPC/DCOM), 172.16.5.20:443 and 172.16.5.25:5682. The fan-out to several internal hosts and the use of remote-management port 5985 are consistent with internal reconnaissance and lateral movement originating from this workstation. Because every one of these connection records shows Owner '-', netscan does not attribute them to a specific process; the responsible process cannot be established from this output alone.
